qa.overall_assessment: Second-pass BA validation: PASS. The prior D5-O1 blocking objection is fully resolved. AC1 now explicitly lists all forbidden forms (two-part positional default, nested default, keyword default), states 'deviates from user's literal text by design', includes a full three-part or-chain regex as the positive check pattern, and adds a negative_pattern that catches any positional-default form. The Technical Rationale section in the ticket provides two numbered reasons for the deviation. All five BA-validation dimensions pass. Two minor codex findings (regex end-anchoring and grep dialect) are noted as non-blocking advisory items.
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: BA correctly identifies agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 10 (line 394) as the underspecified site and DO NOT rule #7 (lines 36-37) as the mismatch-enabling guard. Both sites confirmed by direct Read. Revised AC1 now unambiguously requires the three-part or-chain and explicitly forbids the user-literal two-part form.

prior_blocker_resolution.prior_reason: AC1's verification recipe did not discriminate between the required three-part or-chain and the user-literal two-part default= form. The user-literal deviation was not declared in the AC literal text.

resolution_evidence.evidence: AC1 THEN clause now explicitly states: 'it MUST NOT use os.environ.get(CLAUDE_SESSION_ID, unknown), os.environ.get(CLAUDE_SESSION_ID, default=unknown), os.environ.get(CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, os.environ.get(...)), or any form that passes a default positional or keyword argument to either env lookup'

Technical Rationale section explicitly states 'deviates from user's literal text by design' with two numbered reasons (CLAUDE_SESSION_ID fallback coverage; empty-string safety per Codex finding #2). Forbidden forms are listed with code examples.
AC1 check.pattern is now a full three-part regex requiring exact or-chain form with bare get() calls (no default argument on either lookup).
AC1 negative_pattern added: catches any form with positional default arg on either os.environ.get() call, including the user-literal form.

findings.claim: Phase 10 (line 394) does not specify which env var to use for session_id
findings.detail: Read agents/changelog-analyst.md:390-401 confirms: 'writes a JSON token {commit_sha, branch, repo_root, session_id}' — no env var named. Underspecification confirmed.

findings.claim: DO NOT rule #7 (lines 36-37) uses vague 'current session'
findings.detail: Read agents/changelog-analyst.md:36 confirms text: 'if the token path already exists and its session_id differs from the current session, print a WARNING and skip the token write'. No specific env var referenced. Confirmed vague.

findings.claim: hooks/push.sh (lines 197-232) compares only commit_sha, not session_id
findings.detail: Read hooks/push.sh:197-232 confirms: Python inline script loads token JSON, extracts commit_sha only, compares against HEAD SHA. No session_id field is read or compared. Claim fully accurate.
Glob for scripts/write-push-gate-token.py in /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude returned no results. Confirmed absent.

findings.claim: agents/changelog-analyst.md:75 uses CLAUDE_SESSION_ID for manifest lookup
CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID is present in the environment and inherited by subagents
findings.detail: BA states env | grep CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID returned 96c8ad3a-d367-49a2-87e5-c29ec88e2947. This QA run cannot reproduce the same live env, but the claim that CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID is a stable orchestrator env var set by Claude Code at startup is consistent with known Claude Code behavior. The fallback chain in the spec already handles the case where it is absent.

mosow_alignment.detail: User requirement is: fix agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 10 and overwrite guard to use CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID. BA's Must Have covers both the Phase 10 token writer and the DO NOT rule #7 overwrite guard. The Won't Have exclusions (hooks/push.sh, scripts/execute-push.py, line 75) are consistent with what the user did NOT ask to change.

findings.finding: BA explicitly flags diagnosis_completeness as 'incomplete (partially inferred — runtime reproduction of double-commit scenario not performed)'
findings.assessment: Acceptable for SMALL-tier prose-only change. The logical chain (subagent gets fresh CLAUDE_SESSION_ID per dispatch → second dispatch session_id differs from first → DO NOT rule #7 fires → write skipped → stale token → push fails) is mechanically sound and does not require a live reproduction to validate. The BA's self-disclosure is honest.

agents/changelog-analyst.md.line_394_phase10: confirmed underspecified — no env var named for session_id

dimension_5_spec_text_vs_execution_drift.prior_blocker_d5_o1: RESOLVED — AC1 now explicitly forbids the user-literal two-part form, uses a full three-part or-chain regex for positive check, and adds a negative_pattern catching any positional-default form. Technical Rationale section documents the deviation with two numbered reasons.

remaining_observations.description: AC1 check.pattern is not end-anchored; an unconventional conditional expression suffix would produce a false positive. Codex ISSUE 1.
remaining_observations.codex_proposed_fix: Add \s*(?:#.*)?$ anchor and specify method: 'grep -P' for positive pattern.
remaining_observations.rationale: The file being grepped is a prose markdown file, not Python source. An unconditional assignment form in technical documentation is not a realistic failure mode. The pattern correctly matches the three-part chain as written.

remaining_observations.description: AC1 negative_pattern uses (?:...) which is PCRE syntax; plain grep will fail; grep -E may warn. Method field says 'grep' without dialect specification. Codex ISSUE 2.
remaining_observations.codex_proposed_fix: Either specify 'method': 'grep -P', or rewrite negative_pattern as ERE: PUSH_GATE_SID[[:space:]]*=.*os\.environ\.get\(["'](CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID|CLAUDE_SESSION_ID)["'][[:space:]]*,
remaining_observations.rationale: The semantic correctness of the negative pattern is not in question — it correctly identifies the forbidden positional-default forms. A QA verifier must use grep -P or Python re to run the check. Editorial gap, not semantic.

diagnosis_correctness.detail: The root cause chain is fully confirmed by source inspection: (1) Phase 10 line 394 does not name an env var for session_id — subagent proximity to CLAUDE_SESSION_ID (line 75) makes the ephemeral subagent SID the natural choice. (2) DO NOT rule #7 lines 36-37 uses vague 'current session' — in a second /commit dispatch, the new subagent has a different CLAUDE_SESSION_ID, so the guard fires and skips the token write. (3) push.sh then reports 'no session commit token found' or a sha_mismatch on the stale token. The logical chain is sound.
diagnosis_correctness.failure_mode_confirmed: Token not written (overwrite guard skip) — NOT a session_id comparison in push.sh. push.sh only compares commit_sha.

fix_scope_assessment.detail: The fix targets only agents/changelog-analyst.md at two precisely identified locations (Phase 10 near line 394, DO NOT rule #7 at lines 36-37). The exclusion of hooks/push.sh, scripts/execute-push.py, and line 75 are all justified: push.sh does not compare session_id; execute-push.py is not on the path; line 75 is a separate concern (dispatch manifest lookup). This is minimal and correct.

ac1.detail: THEN clause explicitly names the required form AND all forbidden forms (two-part positional default, nested default, keyword default). check.pattern is a full three-part or-chain regex. negative_pattern catches positional-default forms. D5-O1 resolved. Two minor grep-dialect/anchoring notes are non-blocking.
ac1.prior_gap_resolved: Prior gap was: AC1 did not explicitly require the three-part chain or document the user-literal deviation. Resolved: three-part chain is now mandatory per THEN clause with explicit forbidden-forms list.

ac2.detail: AC2 requires both token write and overwrite guard to reference the same PUSH_GATE_SID. This is verifiable by read and is the key correctness property. Sufficient.

ac3.detail: AC3 uses git diff to verify the minimal-change invariant. The files_must_not_change list is correct. Line 75 unchanged check is explicitly stated. Sufficient.

all_findings.issue: Positive regex not end-anchored; a conditional suffix (e.g. '... or "unknown" if cond else "bad"') would produce a false positive. Risk is theoretical for prose markdown context. Codex ISSUE 1.
all_findings.recommendation: Add \s*(?:#.*)?$ anchor and specify method: 'grep -P'. Low priority for a prose markdown file.

all_findings.issue: (?:...) in negative_pattern requires PCRE; plain grep fails. Method field says 'grep' without dialect qualifier. Codex ISSUE 2.
all_findings.recommendation: Specify method: 'grep -P' or rewrite as ERE: PUSH_GATE_SID[[:space:]]*=.*os\.environ\.get\(["'](CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID|CLAUDE_SESSION_ID)["'][[:space:]]*,

summary.approve_reason: D5-O1 blocking objection from first pass is fully resolved. AC1 now unambiguously requires the three-part or-chain, explicitly forbids all known forbidden forms with named examples, and documents the user-literal deviation with two numbered rationale items. AC2 and AC3 are correct and sufficient. Two minor grep-dialect advisory notes from codex are non-blocking.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed D5-O1 blocker is resolved ('I would not re-raise the original D5-O1 blocker'). Raised two minor issues: (1) positive regex not end-anchored — false-positive risk for conditional expression suffix; (2) negative_pattern uses (?:...) PCRE syntax but method field says grep without dialect qualifier. Both have concrete proposed fixes.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Both codex findings classified as in_scope_minor and recorded as D5-NOTE-1 and D5-NOTE-2 in all_findings. Neither blocks the BA spec from proceeding to dev. Notes added to dimension_5 remaining_observations for future BA authoring awareness.
